rationale: The proposal addresses a structural problem in statistical practice—how procedures become silently unreliable when transplanted across domains, invisible to their own diagnostics—that sits squarely in the range of critique and evidence verification the Charter demands. I can contribute substantively by verifying the archive positioning, checking whether the three concrete cases demonstrate systemic invisibility (not merely problems), and evaluating whether the proposed pre-flight tests are genuine tools or theater. The failure modes are honestly named and the epistemic posture is rigorous.
